mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE increases exceed 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based demand contraction that suppresses corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 45% across multiple recessionary and expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side shocks and inventory drawdowns that trigger short-term equity sell-offs within the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat in over 60% of instances where INDPRO drops exceed 0.5%, invalidating the short-term supply shock transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising core inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate forces markets to price in prolonged monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.4% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS stability shows a positive or null coefficient in out-of-sample regressions predicting 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue bases, creating a structural upward bias in S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns exhibit a negative or statistically insignificant relationship with PAYEMS growth over any 4-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP exceeding 0.2% confirms an economic downturn that rapidly reprices equity risk premiums, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 0.2%, breaking the recession confirmation signal.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during periods of high unemployment indicates a supply-led recovery that outpaces labor market weakness, generating positive equity returns as capacity utilization improves.
failure_condition: The positive predictive power of rising INDPRO during high UNRATE periods vanishes, with 20-day forward returns showing no significant outperformance versus baseline in walk-forward tests.
